title: potentilla incana var. glandulosa
intro: Potentilla incana var. glandulosa, also known as the glandular cinquefoil, is a perennial plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to mountainous regions in the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent qualities. The health benefits of Potentilla incana var. glandulosa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for use in herbal remedies, and it is also sometimes used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
